steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank fills 'the viewer can easily ______ familiar objects.' The contrast is with Embrace, which is abstract and hard to read. In realistic pieces, the viewer has no trouble doing this one thing with familiar objects — the word must mean to recognize or make out what those objects are.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'reveal': Reveal means to uncover or disclose something hidden — viewers are not disclosing objects, they are recognizing them. ✗
  • Choice B — 'remember': Remember implies recalling from memory — the contrast is about perceiving what is depicted, not recalling a past experience. ✗
  • Choice C — 'ignore': The opposite of what is needed — easy perception, not disregard. ✗
  • Choice D — 'identify': Identify means to recognize or determine what something is — exactly what a viewer does when realistic detail makes familiar objects clear and easy to read. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
